value.found_quote: Permission profiles let you apply least-privilege boundaries to local commands Codex runs on your behalf. A profile is a named policy that combines filesystem rules, which define what commands can read or write, with network rules, which define which destinations commands can reach.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Permission profiles as named least-privilege policies combining filesystem and network rules for local commands Codex runs, matching the claim. The page states "Beta. Permission profiles are under active development and may change," indicating beta lifecycle status.
